standard: Community Reinvestment Act — 12 CFR Part 25 / 228 / 345 / 563e — Documentation and Record Retention Requirements
description: Mapping of CRA documentation, record retention, and operational controls to AWS Config managed rules and GuardDuty finding types. Supports OCC/FDIC/Federal Reserve examination readiness for CRA performance evaluation.

controls.description: The bank must maintain a public file containing required CRA information. Data integrity controls must ensure the public file has not been altered or corrupted since its last update.
CRA performance data, HMDA data, and loan records transmitted to examiners or stored in cloud systems must be protected in transit. TLS required for all CRA-related data transmission.
Access to CRA loan data, HMDA data, and community development activity records must be restricted to authorized personnel. Root-level unrestricted access must not exist.

controls.control_name: HMDA/CRA Data — MFA for Authorized Access
controls.description: Personnel accessing CRA systems and HMDA data repositories must authenticate using multi-factor authentication to prevent unauthorized modification of examination records.
All access to and modifications of CRA-related data must be logged to support OCC/FDIC examination requests. Audit trail must be continuous and tamper-evident.
CRA loan files, community development records, and HMDA data stored in databases must be encrypted at rest to prevent unauthorized disclosure to third parties.

controls.control_name: CRA Public Data — No Public Write Access
controls.description: CRA performance data and public file materials stored in cloud systems must be protected against unauthorized modification. Public write access to storage buckets must be prohibited.
Network traffic to systems containing CRA data must be logged and monitored. VPC flow logging enables detection of unauthorized access attempts against CRA record systems.
Automated threat detection must be active on all systems that store or process CRA data to support continuous monitoring requirements during OCC/FDIC examination cycles.
Databases containing CRA loan data, community development records, or HMDA submissions must not be publicly accessible. Publicly accessible RDS instances holding CRA data are a regulatory violation.
API endpoints used to submit, retrieve, or transmit CRA data must enforce TLS. Unencrypted API traffic handling CRA records is a control deficiency.
S3 buckets used to store CRA evidence packages, public file documents, or examination materials must not allow public read access.
